questions.q: Who was Rama's father?
questions.explanation: Rama was the eldest son of King Dasharatha of Ayodhya.

questions.q: Of which god is Rama considered an incarnation (avatar)?
questions.explanation: Rama is revered as the seventh avatar of Vishnu, born to destroy Ravana.

questions.q: Who was Rama's wife?
questions.explanation: Rama married Sita, the daughter of King Janaka of Mithila.

questions.q: Which demon-king did Rama incarnate to destroy?
questions.explanation: Rama was born as an incarnation of Vishnu to slay the demon-king Ravana of Lanka.

questions.q: Which kingdom did Rama belong to as a prince?
questions.explanation: Rama was a prince of the kingdom of Kosala, whose capital was Ayodhya.

questions.q: What is the emblem most associated with Rama?
questions.explanation: Rama is famously depicted as a master archer, and the bow is his signature weapon.

questions.q: Who was Rama's mother?
questions.explanation: Rama was born to Kaushalya, the eldest queen of Dasharatha.

questions.q: Which brother accompanied Rama and Sita into the forest exile?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana, Rama's devoted brother, chose to share his exile and served him throughout.

questions.q: Rama is upheld in the epic as the ideal of which virtue?
questions.explanation: Rama is celebrated as the ideal of dharma, righteous conduct and duty.

questions.q: How many years was Rama exiled to the forest?
questions.explanation: Rama accepted fourteen years of forest exile to honour his father's word.

questions.q: Which queen's two boons forced Rama into exile?
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi used two boons from Dasharatha to demand the throne for Bharata and exile for Rama.

questions.q: Which sage took the young Rama to guard his sacrifice and taught him celestial weapons?
questions.explanation: The warrior-sage Vishvamitra took Rama and Lakshmana to protect his sacrifice and imparted divine weapons.

questions.q: What deed did Rama perform to win the hand of Sita at her swayamvara?
options: Defeated a hundred kings in combat
questions.explanation: Rama lifted and strung Shiva's mighty bow Pinaka, snapping it in two and winning Sita.

questions.q: Whom did Rama free from a curse simply by the touch or dust of his feet on the way to Mithila?
questions.explanation: Ahalya, cursed to be stone, was restored to her form by the grace of Rama.

questions.q: Which fearsome demoness did Rama slay as his first great deed at Vishvamitra's bidding?
questions.explanation: At Vishvamitra's command Rama slew the demoness Tataka, freeing the forest hermitages.

questions.q: Which warrior-sage avatar of Vishnu did Rama confront after breaking Shiva's bow?
questions.explanation: Parashurama challenged Rama after the bow broke; Rama drew Parashurama's own bow and the elder yielded.

questions.q: What did Rama give to Bharata to rule Ayodhya in his place during the exile?
questions.explanation: Rama gave Bharata his wooden sandals (paduka), which Bharata enthroned and ruled in their name.

questions.q: Which vanara king did Rama ally with to search for the abducted Sita?
questions.explanation: Rama allied with Sugriva, the exiled vanara king, who lent his host to find Sita.

questions.q: Which lured Rama away from the hut so that Ravana could abduct Sita?
questions.explanation: The rakshasa Maricha took the form of a golden deer to draw Rama away from Sita.

questions.q: Whom did Rama slay with an arrow from concealment to restore Sugriva to the throne?
questions.explanation: Rama struck the vanara king Vali from hiding during his duel with Sugriva, a deed long debated for its dharma.

questions.q: Which weapon did Rama use to finally slay Ravana?
questions.explanation: Rama loosed the irresistible Brahmastra, piercing Ravana through the heart.

questions.q: Whom did Rama promise the throne of Lanka after the war?
questions.explanation: Rama promised the kingdom of Lanka to Vibhishana, Ravana's righteous brother who joined his cause.

questions.q: From which dynasty did Rama descend?
questions.explanation: Rama belonged to the solar Ikshvaku dynasty, whose family preceptor was the sage Vasishtha.

questions.q: Which sage in the Dandaka forest gifted Rama the bow and quiver of Vishnu?
questions.explanation: The sage Agastya received Rama in the Dandaka forest and gave him divine weapons including the bow of Vishnu.

questions.q: In how many kandas (books) is the Ramayana, the epic of Rama, traditionally told?
questions.explanation: The Valmiki Ramayana narrating Rama's life is traditionally told in seven kandas.

questions.q: Which demon, cousin of Ravana, led an army of fourteen thousand that Rama destroyed alone at Janasthana?
questions.explanation: Khara led fourteen thousand rakshasas to avenge Shurpanakha, and Rama destroyed them all single-handedly.

questions.q: What honorific title is given to Rama as the man who never transgressed the bounds of virtue?
questions.explanation: Rama is called Maryada Purushottam, the perfect man who never crossed the limits of virtue and duty.

questions.q: Whose dying words told Rama which direction Sita had been carried after her abduction?
questions.explanation: The mortally wounded vulture Jatayu lived just long enough to tell Rama the direction Ravana took Sita.

questions.q: After fourteen years, Rama's return to Ayodhya is traditionally remembered as the origin of which festival?
questions.explanation: The people lit rows of lamps to welcome Rama home, an event remembered ever after as Diwali.

questions.q: By what name is the just and prosperous reign of Rama as king of Ayodhya remembered?
questions.explanation: Rama's ideal reign is remembered as Rama Rajya, a byword for perfect, righteous rule.
